installHint.title: Use Avibe like an app
installHint.body: Add it to your Home Screen so the keyboard stops covering the input, with no browser bars and a more native feel.
installHint.step1: Tap the Share button at the bottom

errorBoundary.title: Something went wrong
errorBoundary.body: This part of the app hit an unexpected error. The rest still works — retry, or reload the page.

preview.title: Preview
preview.failed: Couldn't load this file for preview.
preview.tooLarge: File too large to preview — download it instead.
preview.truncated: Large file — showing only the first part.

common.copyFailed: Copy failed, please copy it manually
common.proxyUrlHint: SOCKS5, SOCKS4, HTTP or HTTPS proxy. Leave empty to fall back to system SOCKS proxy detection or direct connection.
common.proxyUrlCollapsedHint: (blank → system proxy if set, else direct)

more.title: More
more.controlPanelDesc: Dashboard · Remote Access · Messaging Platforms · Backends · Advanced Settings

newSession.title: New session
newSession.placeholder: Describe what you want the agent to do…
newSession.loadError: Couldn't load projects — close and reopen to retry.
newSession.createFailed: Couldn't start the session. Please try again.

showPages.title: Show Pages
showPages.subtitle: Every agent session has a Show Page. Make it public, keep it private, or take it offline.
showPages.empty: No Show Pages yet. They appear here once an agent session creates one.
showPages.emptyFiltered: No Show Pages match this filter.

icon.hint: SVG, PNG, ICO, JPEG, or WebP · up to 2 MiB. Shown in the Dock and App Library.

showPages.offlineNoLink: This page is offline — bring it back with the control above to get a live link.
showPages.rotateHint: Generates a new link and disables the current one.

shareId.label: Custom link
shareId.hint: 3–64 letters, numbers, dash, or underscore. Must be unique.

errors.taken: That custom link is already taken. Pick another.
errors.invalid: Use 3–64 letters, numbers, dash, or underscore, starting and ending with a letter or number.

showPages.cloudOff: Avibe Cloud is off — this link only opens on this device.

settings.serviceSubtitle: Manage runtime status, console server details, and remote access from one place.
settings.serviceRuntimeSubtitle: Start, stop, and restart the local Avibe service while checking the live process state.
settings.consoleServerHint: Saving these values restarts only the Web UI server.
settings.instanceNameHint: Shown in the browser tab title ("Avibe - name"). Leave blank to use the remote-access name when paired, otherwise the system hostname.
settings.consoleServerRelocated: UI server now listens on {{origin}}. Open this URL to continue.
settings.serviceNotesSubtitle: Keep the local service healthy before moving on to platform and routing work.
settings.serviceNote1: Remote access is now part of Service because it changes how this machine exposes the local admin UI.
settings.serviceNote2: Host and port changes restart only the UI server, while service control buttons affect the main Python runtime.
settings.serviceNote3: Use Diagnostics for doctor checks and Live logs when you need detailed troubleshooting evidence.
settings.platformsSubtitle: Configure which messaging platforms are connected and how each one is enabled.
settings.backendsSubtitle: Manage agent backends, default routing, and local CLI availability.
settings.dependenciesSubtitle: Local runtimes that Avibe installs and keeps up to date automatically.

dependencies.autoBanner: Avibe installs and updates these for you. Re-check, repair, or update manually below.
dependencies.backendsDetail: Claude · OpenCode · Codex — managed in the Backends tab.

askill.label: askill
askill.detail: Agent Skills runtime — powers the Skills page.

avault.label: avault
avault.detail: Vault custody core — required for local Vault operations.

show-runtime.label: Show Page runtime
show-runtime.detail: Renders visual Show Pages on your machine.

tmux.label: tmux
tmux.detail: Persistent terminal runtime for reconnectable Web Terminal sessions.

node.label: Node.js
node.detail: Shared prerequisite for the runtimes above.

backends.routingHint: Pick the backend that powers new conversations by default. Per-group overrides are configured on the Groups page.
backends.cliPathHint: Avibe runs this command for every request. Use an absolute path if the binary is not on $PATH.
backends.opencodeSubtitle: Detect or install the OpenCode CLI and apply the permission profile that lets it skip approval prompts.
backends.opencodeDescription: Multi-provider open agent · CLI binary detection + permission profile
backends.claudeSubtitle: Detect or install the Claude Code CLI. Sign in or supply an API key from the CLI itself.
backends.claudeDescription: Anthropic agent · sign in with Claude or supply an API key
backends.codexDescription: OpenAI agent · ChatGPT login or OPENAI_API_KEY
backends.codexSubtitle: Switch between ChatGPT login and OpenAI API key, and point Codex at a custom endpoint when needed.
backends.codexAuthModeOauthHint: Use a ChatGPT account to authorize Codex. Avibe stops managing the API key while this is selected.
backends.codexAuthModeApiKeyHint: Codex will call the OpenAI-compatible endpoint with the key below. Switch to ChatGPT login if you prefer not to manage a key.
backends.codexApiKeyConfigured: A key of length {{length}} is currently stored.
backends.codexBaseUrlHint: Leave empty to use OpenAI's default endpoint. Compatible relays (e.g. Azure OpenAI, OpenRouter) accept their own URL here.
backends.codexHasChatgptTokens: Codex has cached ChatGPT credentials from a previous sign-in.
backends.codexOauthSignedIn: Codex is signed in via ChatGPT.
backends.codexOauthPanelTitle: Sign in with your ChatGPT account
backends.codexOauthPanelSubtitle: Open the device URL below, enter the code, and finish login in your browser. Codex will detect completion automatically.
backends.codexDeviceInstructions: Open the URL above, paste the device code, and complete sign-in in your browser. This page updates automatically when Codex receives the callback.
backends.codexCredentialsStoreKeyringWarn: Codex is currently set to store credentials in the OS keychain ({{store}}). Saving here will switch it to file storage so Avibe can manage the API key directly.
backends.codexAuthModeUncertain: Codex is set to store credentials in the OS keychain ({{store}}), so the current auth state cannot be read here. Choose a mode below and save — Avibe will switch Codex to file storage so future reads stay accurate.
backends.codexInfoHint: Saving here writes `~/.codex/auth.json` and `~/.codex/config.toml`, then restarts the persistent app-server so the next request uses the new settings.
backends.claudeAuthModeOauthHint: Use your Anthropic account to authorize Claude Code. Avibe stops managing the API key while this is selected.
backends.claudeAuthModeApiKeyHint: Saving updates Claude Code's own `settings.json` env block so the CLI uses this key directly.
backends.claudeOauthPanelTitle: Sign in with your Anthropic account
backends.claudeOauthPanelSubtitle: Open the authorization URL below, approve access, then paste the callback code Anthropic shows you.
backends.claudeCallbackCodeHint: Anthropic shows a value like `abc123#xyz`. Paste it here exactly — Avibe forwards it to Claude Code.
backends.claudeApiKeyConfigured: A key of length {{length}} is currently stored.
backends.claudeBaseUrlHint: Leave empty to use Anthropic's default endpoint. Compatible relays (e.g. Bedrock proxies, AWS endpoints) accept their own URL here.
backends.claudeSettingsConflictTitle: Legacy Avibe key will be migrated
backends.claudeSettingsConflictBody: Saving updates `{{var}}` in {{path}} and clears the old Avibe copy.
backends.claudeInfoHint: Saving here writes Claude Code's `settings.json` env values. Claude relaunches per request, so no Avibe restart is needed.
backends.claudeSavePartial: API key saved, but old Claude account credentials were not fully cleared: {{detail}}
backends.oauthPollTimedOut: Login did not complete within the allowed time. Please retry.
backends.oauthRemovePartial: Credentials cleared from Avibe, but the backend CLI logout reported a problem: {{detail}}. The CLI's own files may still be present.
backends.oauthFlowLockedToast: OAuth login is in progress — finish or cancel it before switching auth mode.
backends.testConnectionSubtitle: Send a one-token "Hi" prompt to verify the session, the model, and any custom Base URL.
backends.testFailureInvalidCredentials: The stored credentials were rejected (401). Replace your API key or re-authenticate.
backends.testFailureForbidden: The endpoint accepted the credentials but denied access (403). Check the relay's allowed-models list.
backends.testFailureModelNotFound: The model the backend tried to use was not recognised by the endpoint. Check the model id or relay routing.
backends.testFailureRateLimited: The endpoint returned rate-limited / quota-exceeded (429). Wait or rotate to a key with higher limits.
backends.testFailureEndpointUnreachable: Could not reach the configured Base URL. Check DNS, proxy, and that the relay is up.
backends.testFailureServerError: The endpoint responded with a 5xx error. The relay is reachable but not healthy.
backends.testFailureTrustCheck: Codex refused to run from the current directory. Restart the regression container so the cwd is trusted.
backends.testFailureCliNotFound: The backend CLI binary was not found. Check the Path on the runtime card.
backends.testFailureSpawnFailed: Could not launch the backend CLI: {{detail}}
backends.testFailureTimedOut: The CLI did not respond within the test timeout.
backends.testFailureNotLoggedIn: The backend CLI did not find a signed-in account. Sign in again, then rerun the test.
backends.testFailureCliFailed: The CLI exited with an error. Expand the raw output below to see what it said.
backends.claudeApiKeyRemoveConfirm: Remove the stored Anthropic API key? OAuth credentials are left untouched.
backends.codexApiKeyRemoveConfirm: Remove the stored OpenAI API key from auth.json? Codex will fall back to OAuth tokens if present, or sit unconfigured if not.
backends.codexNoticeClearedRelayPointer: Cleared the custom relay pointer ({{provider}} → {{url}}) because OAuth tokens won't validate against a custom base URL. Codex will now use OpenAI's default endpoint. To switch back, re-add an API key or edit ~/.codex/config.toml manually.
backends.opencodeProviderOauthPanelSubtitle: Open the URL, complete login in your browser; the OpenCode daemon detects completion automatically.
backends.opencodeCallbackUrlHint: After authorizing, your browser will land on `http://127.0.0.1:<port>/callback?code=...` — copy that exact URL from the address bar and paste it here. Avibe replays it inside the container so OpenCode's listener receives the credential.
backends.opencodeProvidersSubtitle: Lazy-loaded from the OpenCode server · auth handled via API key or OAuth sign-in
backends.opencodeDisabledBanner: Enable OpenCode in the header to manage providers. The auth-mode toggle is the only interactive control while the backend is off.
backends.opencodeCustomProviderSubtitle: Create an OpenAI-compatible or Anthropic-compatible provider backed by OpenCode config.
backends.opencodeCustomProviderRequired: Name, Provider ID, and Base URL are required.
backends.opencodeCustomProviderIdInvalid: Provider ID must start with a lowercase letter or number and use only lowercase letters, numbers, dot, hyphen, or underscore.
backends.opencodeCustomProviderBaseUrlInvalid: Base URL must start with http:// or https://.
backends.opencodeCustomProviderRemoveConfirm: Delete custom provider {{name}}? This removes its config, models, and stored credentials.
backends.opencodeDefaultNeedsKey: {{name}} has no API key yet — set one to make it the default.
backends.opencodeDefaultSaveFailed: Could not change the default provider.
backends.opencodeProvidersEmpty: OpenCode returned no providers. Run `opencode auth list` from a terminal to diagnose.
backends.opencodeProvidersFilterEmpty: No providers match the current search or filter.
backends.opencodeProvidersError: Could not load the OpenCode provider catalog.
backends.opencodeProvidersInfo: Provider data is fetched live from the OpenCode server.
backends.opencodeProviderModelsEmpty: No models yet. Add a model ID above to use this provider.
backends.opencodeProviderModelHint: User-added models are written to OpenCode config and appear in this provider's test dropdown.
backends.opencodeProviderModelRequired: Enter a model ID before adding it.
backends.opencodeProviderModelNoProviderPrefix: Enter only the model ID here, without the provider/ prefix.
backends.opencodeProviderModelDuplicate: That model ID already exists for this provider.
backends.opencodeProviderApiKeyPlaceholderStored: Enter a new key to replace the stored one
backends.opencodeProviderApiKeyStored: A key is currently stored on disk.
backends.opencodeProviderApiKeyMissing: No API key stored for this provider yet.
backends.opencodeProviderApiKeyRequired: Enter the provider's API key before saving.
backends.opencodeProviderBaseUrlHint: Provider-level overrides go in `~/.config/opencode/opencode.json`. Leave empty to use the provider's default.
backends.opencodeProviderBaseUrlRequired: Base URL is required for custom providers.
backends.opencodeProviderOauthHint: You can also run `opencode auth login {{id}}` in a terminal to authenticate without storing a key here.
backends.opencodeProviderLocalHint: Local providers do not need credentials — start the runtime and load a model.
backends.opencodeProviderCatalogRefreshPending: Provider saved. Refreshing the model list…
backends.opencodeProviderRemoveOauthConfirm: Sign out of {{name}}? This removes the stored OAuth credentials.
backends.opencodeTestSubtitle: Send a one-token "Hi" through OpenCode using this provider only.
backends.testFailureOpencodeServerUnavailable: OpenCode's local server is not reachable. Start it from the Runtime page and retry.
backends.testFailureNoModels: This provider has not reported any models yet. Save a key first, or open the provider catalog.
backends.testFailureSessionCreate: OpenCode rejected the test session — check the server logs for a deeper error.
backends.testFailureMissingProvider: Provider id is missing — reload the page and try again.

settings.messagingSubtitle: Tune global reply behavior, acknowledgements, and message visibility defaults.
settings.messagingCardDescription: Fine-tune how Avibe acknowledges work, retries failures, and formats user-facing replies.
settings.messagingInputEnrichmentDescription: Transform inbound user content before it reaches an agent.
settings.messagingAgentContextDescription: Metadata prepended to each user turn sent to an agent.
settings.messagingWorkFeedbackDescription: How Avibe acknowledges, retries, and reports running work.
settings.messagingReplyExperienceDescription: Controls for messages Avibe sends back into chat.
settings.messagingDisplayDescription: Tune how conversations are rendered in the Web UI.
settings.messagingGroupsDescription: Jump to group allow-list and per-group backend routing.
settings.messagingGroupsHint: Manage which groups are allowed and how each one routes to an agent backend.
settings.messagingSupportLine1: Typing indicators and reaction-based acknowledgements depend on the capabilities of the platforms you enabled.
settings.messagingSupportLine2: Slack-specific link preview suppression is shown only when Slack is part of the active platform set.
settings.messagingSupportLine3: For per-group overrides, open the Groups page and change routing there instead of relying on the global defaults alone.
settings.diagnosticsSubtitle: Run health checks, inspect warnings, and jump into live logs when something is off.
settings.logsSubtitle: Inspect the runtime log stream with filters, search, and source selection.
settings.inProgressTitle: This settings page is next in the redesign queue
settings.inProgressBody: The new route and shell are live, but the feature-specific UI for this section is still being migrated from the old dashboard and wizard flows.
settings.inProgressHint: Until this section is rebuilt, the setup wizard still exposes the underlying configuration paths.

remoteAccess.title: Remote Access
remoteAccess.subtitle: Open this local Web UI from another device through a private avibe.bot URL. Your data and agents still run on this machine.
remoteAccess.subtitleWithLink: Reach your Avibe OS from anywhere through your own *-app.avibe.bot domain. Your data and agents stay under your control on this machine.
remoteAccess.connectDesc: First create a remote-access bot at avibe.bot and copy its one-time pairing key. Then paste it here to pair this device.
remoteAccess.configuredDesc: This machine has saved the remote-access credentials. The pairing key was one-time use; after restart, Avibe uses the saved connection credentials.
remoteAccess.configuredHelp: Use re-pair only when switching account, changing domain, or regenerating remote access.
remoteAccess.legacyHostnameTitle: This remote address needs to be replaced
remoteAccess.legacyHostnameDesc: Older instances used *.remote.avibe.bot. That is a nested hostname and may fail TLS on Cloudflare Universal SSL. Create a new remote-access bot at avibe.bot, then re-pair here to receive a *.avibe.bot address.
remoteAccess.pairingKeyHelp: The pairing key is shown once and usually starts with vrp_. After redemption, Avibe stores connection credentials, not this one-time key.
remoteAccess.flowStep1: Open <console>avibe.bot</console>, sign in, click “Pair an Avibe”, and claim your own domain.
remoteAccess.flowStep2: Copy the pairing key shown there.
remoteAccess.flowStep3: Paste it here to finish pairing — then reach Avibe from anywhere.
remoteAccess.optimizeStarted: A second Connector is evaluating a better route

accessBlocked.title: Can't open the control panel here
accessBlocked.body: Avibe is running, but it refused to open the control panel at this address.
accessBlocked.hint: Open Avibe from your https://<your-slug>.avibe.bot link, or use http://127.0.0.1:5123 on the machine running Avibe. A raw LAN or Tailscale IP is blocked while remote access is on.

errors.share_id_taken: That custom link is already taken. Pick another.
errors.invalid_share_id: A custom link may use only 3–64 letters, numbers, dash, or underscore, starting and ending with a letter or number.
errors.not_public: Make the Show Page public first to change its link.
errors.session_archived: This session is archived, so its Show Page can't be changed.
errors.invalid_icon_type: The icon must be an SVG, PNG, ICO, JPEG, or WebP image.
errors.icon_too_large: That icon is too large. Use an image up to 2 MiB.
errors.icon_required: Choose an icon file to upload.
errors.icon_write_failed: Couldn't save the icon. Please try again.
errors.project_no_folder: This project has no local folder configured.
errors.remote_access_host_mismatch: This address can't open the Avibe control panel. Open it from your https://<your-slug>.avibe.bot link, or http://127.0.0.1:5123 on the machine running Avibe.
errors.remote_access_config_unavailable: Avibe couldn't read its configuration. Check that the service is healthy and try again.
errors.remote_access_public_url_invalid: Remote access is misconfigured (the public URL is invalid). Re-pair this device or fix the remote-access settings.
errors.remote_access_session_secret_missing: Remote access is misconfigured (missing session secret). Re-pair this device to restore access.
errors.remote_access_unknown: The operation did not finish. Refresh the status and try again; if it still fails, check the remote access runtime logs.
errors.invalid_pairing_key: This pairing key is invalid or expired. Create a new key at avibe.bot, then paste it here.
errors.invalid_pairing_backend_url: The Avibe Cloud URL must be a valid HTTPS address.
errors.pairing_backend_url_not_allowed: That Avibe Cloud URL is not allowed. Use a public HTTPS avibe.bot address.
errors.pairing_backend_unresolvable: Avibe could not resolve that Avibe Cloud URL. Check the address and try again.
errors.backend_http_redirect_blocked: Avibe Cloud redirected the pairing request, so Avibe stopped before sending the pairing key.
errors.pairing_request_failed: Could not connect to avibe.bot. Check the network connection and make sure the Avibe Cloud URL is correct.
errors.backend_http_error: avibe.bot rejected this request. Try again later, or create a new pairing key.
errors.invalid_pairing_response: avibe.bot returned incomplete pairing data. Refresh the page, create a new pairing key, and try again.
errors.remote_access_disabled: Remote access is not enabled yet. Pair this device first.
errors.missing_tunnel_token: Pairing data is incomplete because the Tunnel credential is missing. Pair this device again.
errors.cloudflared_install_failed: Connector installation failed. Check network access and local write permissions, then try again.
errors.cloudflared_spawn_failed: Connector startup failed. Check that cloudflared is executable, or inspect the runtime logs.
errors.cloudflared_exited: The connector exited immediately after startup. This usually means the Tunnel credential is invalid or the Cloudflare config is not ready. Pair again and retry.
errors.cloudflared_stop_failed: The connector could not be stopped. Try again later, or manually stop the cloudflared process.
errors.cloudflared_process_unknown: Avibe found a connector-like process but could not verify ownership. To avoid killing the wrong process, it did not change it. Check the cloudflared process manually and retry.
errors.remote_access_not_running: Start Remote Access before optimizing its route.
errors.route_optimization_unavailable: A route optimization is already running or cooling down.
errors.route_optimization_start_failed: Avibe could not start route optimization. Check the remote access runtime logs.
errors.bind_code_limit_reached: Too many active bind codes. Revoke an unused code before creating another.

editor.label: Editor
editor.desc: A fast code editor for files on this machine.
editor.tagline: A fast code editor, in your browser.
editor.welcomeSub: A code editor for files on this machine · VS Code engine
editor.selectFileHint: Select a file from the explorer to edit
editor.emptyHint: Open a file from Files to edit it here.
editor.saveBeforePopOut: Save before opening in a window
editor.confirmDiscardClose: Discard unsaved changes and close this window?

conflict.message: This file changed on disk since you opened it. The agent may have edited it.

search.title: Search
search.hint: Type to search across this folder
search.needFolder: Open a folder to search across its files.
search.truncatedMatches: Showing the first 1000 matches — narrow your search to see the rest.
search.truncatedFiles: Showing the first 200 files — narrow your search to see the rest.
search.allSkipped: No files changed — {{n}} skipped (conflicts or write-protected).
search.undoSkipped: Undo left {{n}} file(s) unchanged (modified since the replace).

dock.emptyHint: Dock is empty · Open App Library

showPage.label: Show Page
showPage.missingBody: The Show Page for this session is missing or was archived. It may come back if the session does.

fileBrowser.label: Files
fileBrowser.desc: Browse, view, and edit files on this machine.
fileBrowser.placeholder: The File Browser is being built. You'll be able to browse the whole machine, open files, and edit and save them right here.
fileBrowser.selectHint: Select a file to view or edit it.
fileBrowser.noPreview: No in-app preview for this file type.
fileBrowser.tooLarge: This file is too large to edit in the browser. Download it to view it instead.
fileBrowser.uploadNoFolders: Folders can't be uploaded — drop files only.
fileBrowser.deleteDirHint: The folder and everything inside it will be deleted.
fileBrowser.deleteBatchHint: The selected items and all contents of selected folders will be deleted.
fileBrowser.downloadFoldersSkipped: Skipped {{count}} folder(s); download supports files only.
fileBrowser.moveClashDesc: Something with that name is already in the destination folder.
fileBrowser.moveClashTooMany: Too many copies — couldn't find an available name.
fileBrowser.conflict: This file changed on disk since you opened it. Reload before saving to avoid overwriting it.
fileBrowser.listTruncated: Only the first {{count}} entries are shown.

errors.conflict: This file changed on disk since you opened it. Reload before saving to avoid overwriting it.
errors.file_not_utf8: This file isn't valid UTF-8 text, so it can't be edited safely — download it instead.
errors.not_file: Path is not a regular file.
errors.is_symlink: Symbolic links cannot be edited here.
errors.invalid_response: The server returned an unexpected response. Try again.

terminal.label: Terminal
terminal.desc: A real shell in your browser.
terminal.placeholder: The Terminal arrives in Phase 2: a real shell with tmux-backed persistent sessions.
terminal.tagline: A real shell, in your browser.
terminal.disabled: The terminal is turned off for this service, or this session isn't authorized to use it. Whoever runs the service can turn it on in the service configuration.
terminal.pasteBlocked: Couldn't read the clipboard — check your browser's clipboard permission and try again.

workbench.placeholderTitle: Your AI workbench is coming together
workbench.placeholderBody: Agents, Skills, Harness, and Vaults will live here. For now, jump into the control panel to configure platforms and backends.

canvas.heroBody: Describe what you want and an agent will run it. Or start with one of the suggestions below.

suggestions.newProjectHint: Pick a folder; sessions default to it
suggestions.openHarnessHint: Have an agent write the cron / watch

canvas.inputPlaceholder: Tell the agent what you want…
canvas.noProjectForChat: No projects yet — add one before starting a chat

workbench.projectArchiveConfirm: Archive project "{{name}}"? Session history is preserved; it just stops showing in the sidebar.
workbench.sessionForkUnavailable: This session has no native agent session to fork yet.

archiveSession.title: Archive this session permanently?
archiveSession.body: “{{name}}” will be permanently archived and can't be restored.
archiveSession.previewError: Couldn't verify linked resources — they'll still be permanently deleted.
archiveSession.reclaimIntro: These linked resources will be permanently deleted:
archiveSession.noReclaim: No scheduled tasks or watches are linked to this session.

workbench.projectsEmpty: No projects yet — add one to start a session.

newProjectDialog.title: Open project
newProjectDialog.description: Pick a working directory. Opening a folder you've used before reopens that project — restoring it if it was archived.

search.title: Search
search.footerNote: Apps and message content · all sessions

inbox.title: Inbox
inbox.empty: Nothing in your inbox yet — agent replies will surface here.
inbox.emptyBody: When an agent posts back from a session, it shows up here.
inbox.allClearBody: No unread agent messages right now. Switch to "All" to browse history.
inbox.moreUnreadBeyond: More unread sessions may be further down — load more to see them.

modules.comingSoon: This capability is still on the way — the full surface lands in a later release.

inbox.title: Inbox
inbox.description: Cross-project notifications from every Avibe session. Hover popover + full list view will land in a later commit.

agents.title: Agents
agents.description: Global registry of every Avibe Agent — Claude / OpenCode / Codex variants you can pick when starting a session.

skills.title: Skills
skills.description: Manage Agent Skills (global + project-scoped) on top of the askill CLI.

harness.title: Harness
harness.description: Background tasks, watches, webhooks, and the run history of all three. Created by chatting with an Agent.

vaults.title: Vaults
vaults.description: Secrets and API keys that the Agent can pull into ENV / .env without you copy-pasting.

welcome.title: Welcome to Avibe
welcome.subtitle: This machine is now home to your AI agent. Talk to it from the built-in Workbench — or connect Slack, Discord, Telegram, Lark, and WeChat.
welcome.feature1: Your data + agents stay on your machine.
welcome.feature2: Slack, Discord, Telegram, Lark, WeChat — one place.
welcome.feature3: Claude, OpenCode, Codex — route per channel.

modeSelection.title: Choose Mode
modeSelection.saasDescription: OAuth install + cloud relay + local execution (coming soon).
modeSelection.selfHostDescription: Use your own platform credentials and keep execution local.

platform.title: Where you'll talk to your agent
platform.subtitle: Your Avibe Workbench is always on — a built-in web app you can even add to your phone's home screen. Connect chat platforms like Slack or Telegram too, or skip and set them up later.
platform.workbenchDesc: Your built-in web chat for talking to agents — always on, nothing to set up.
platform.workbenchPwa: Add to your phone's home screen — use it like a native app.
platform.primaryDesc: Used for compatibility ordering and default UI focus.
platform.credentialsSubtitle: Connect each IM platform with the credentials your bot uses to authenticate.
platform.disableConfirmBody: {{name}} will stop receiving messages. You can re-enable it anytime.
platform.applyHint: Apply updates to the credential tabs below, disabled platforms keep saved tokens.
platform.configureBeforeEnable: Configure and save this platform before enabling it.
platform.restartFailed: Configuration saved, but service restart failed
platform.restartInProgress: Saved. The service is busy restarting — toggle again shortly to make sure it takes effect.
platform.credentialSetupSubtitle: Configure the selected platform directly here, then validate credentials before enabling groups.
platform.expandedStepHint: Use this shared setup module for {{platform}} credentials.
platform.credentialGuide: Open the platform console, copy the required credentials, paste them below, then validate the connection.
platform.wechatCredentialHint: WeChat uses QR binding. Rebinding and login status remain in the WeChat setup flow.

slack.title: Slack
slack.desc: Connect a Slack workspace and enable groups.

discord.title: Discord
discord.desc: Connect a Discord bot and choose a guild.

telegram.title: Telegram
telegram.desc: Connect a Telegram bot for DMs, groups, and forum topics.

lark.title: Lark
lark.desc: Connect to Feishu/Lark workspace for team collaboration

wechat.title: WeChat
wechat.desc: Connect to WeChat personal messaging via iLink bot

avibe.title: Avibe Workbench
avibe.desc: Chat with your agents directly in this local web workbench.

discordConfig.title: Discord Configuration
discordConfig.subtitle: Create a Discord app, invite the bot, then add your token.
discordConfig.botTokenHint: Use the token from the Discord Developer Portal.
discordConfig.guild: Guilds where the bot can work
discordConfig.guildHint: This controls which Discord guilds the bot can work in. Configure group enablement and routing in the next step.
discordConfig.step1Description: Create a new application in the Developer Portal.
discordConfig.step1Item3: Open the Bot tab (your bot is created automatically)
discordConfig.step2Description: Enable Message Content Intent in the Bot tab. Permissions are included in the invite link.
discordConfig.step2Item1: In the Bot tab, enable "Message Content Intent" and save
discordConfig.step2Item2: No need to set Bot Permissions here
discordConfig.step2Tip: Message Content Intent is required to read message text.
discordConfig.step3Description: Paste your Client ID and we will generate a ready-to-use OAuth2 invite link.
discordConfig.step3Item1: In your app, open General Information and copy Client ID
discordConfig.step3Item2: Paste it below to generate the invite URL
discordConfig.step3Item3: Permissions are prefilled in the invite URL
discordConfig.step3Item4: Open the invite URL, choose a server, and authorize
discordConfig.clientIdHint: Find this in General Information of your Discord application.
discordConfig.inviteUrlPlaceholder: Invite URL will appear here after Client ID
discordConfig.step4Description: Copy the bot token and validate access.
discordConfig.step4Item1: In the Bot tab, click "Reset Token" or "View Token"
discordConfig.step4Item2: Copy the token and paste it here
discordConfig.step4Item3: Validate the token and choose a guild
discordConfig.dmTroubleshoot1: Make sure your account and the bot are in at least one common server.
discordConfig.dmTroubleshoot2: In server Privacy Settings, enable "Allow direct messages from server members".
discordConfig.dmTroubleshoot3: Ensure you didn't block the bot account.

telegramConfig.title: Telegram Configuration
telegramConfig.subtitle: Create a Telegram bot, validate the token, then finish the group setup in a few guided steps.
telegramConfig.step1Description: Use @BotFather to create a new bot and prepare the API token.
telegramConfig.step1Item3: Choose a display name for the bot
telegramConfig.step1Item4: Choose a username ending in "bot" and keep the generated token page open
telegramConfig.step1Tip: If you already created the bot, you can skip straight to copying the token in Step 2.
telegramConfig.step2Description: Copy the bot token from BotFather and verify that Avibe can talk to the bot.
telegramConfig.step2Item1: In BotFather, open your bot and choose the API token option
telegramConfig.step2Item2: Copy the token and paste it below
telegramConfig.step3Description: Set the BotFather switches that directly control whether the bot can join groups and receive the messages you expect.
telegramConfig.step3Item1: Run the commands below in BotFather and select your bot each time
telegramConfig.step3Item2: If you want normal group messages without @mentions, set privacy mode to Disable
telegramConfig.step3Item3: If the bot should be used in groups or forums, make sure group joining is enabled
telegramConfig.step3Item4: Publish the common slash commands so new users can discover them from the Telegram command picker
telegramConfig.step3Command1Description: Send /setprivacy, choose your bot, then select Disable. Otherwise Telegram forwards only commands, @mentions, and replies to the bot.
telegramConfig.step3Command2Title: Allow the bot to join groups
telegramConfig.step3Command2Description: Send /setjoingroups, choose your bot, and make sure it is set to Enable so the bot can be added to groups and forums normally.
telegramConfig.step3Command3Description: Send /setcommands and add common commands like /start, /settings, /new, and /resume so users immediately see what the bot can do.
telegramConfig.step3Tip: If you expect the bot to respond to normal group messages without explicit @mentions, /setprivacy must be set to Disable. This is the most common Telegram setup miss.
telegramConfig.step3ExtraTip: If you only need the bot in DMs for now, /setjoingroups can wait. But before using groups or forum topics, finish it first.
telegramConfig.step4Title: Finish Setup, Bind, Then Discover Chats
telegramConfig.step4Description: Telegram chat discovery happens only after Avibe is running and has received inbound messages. Finish setup first, then come back from the dashboard to enable discovered group or forum chats.
telegramConfig.step4Item1: Finish the setup flow and start the service
telegramConfig.step4Item2: On the summary screen, copy the first bind command and send `bind <code>` or `/bind <code>` to the bot in DM
telegramConfig.step4Item3: After binding, send /start in DM; for groups or forums, add the bot there and grant message permission (plus admin/topic-management rights if you want forum auto-topic mode)
telegramConfig.step4Item4: Send one message in each target group or forum chat, then refresh the Telegram chat list later from the dashboard group settings page
telegramConfig.step4Tip: Direct messages stay hidden from the selectable Telegram chat list. If the bot still reacts only to commands or @mentions in groups, go back to Step 3 and verify that /setprivacy is really set to Disable.
telegramConfig.step5Description: Set the default group reply behavior and forum topic behavior for this bot.
telegramConfig.botTokenHint: Create a bot with @BotFather, then paste the token here.
telegramConfig.proxyUrlHint: SOCKS5, SOCKS4, HTTP or HTTPS proxy. Leave empty if Telegram is accessible directly.
telegramConfig.requireMention: Require explicit bot targeting in groups
telegramConfig.requireMentionHint: When enabled, the bot responds in groups only when explicitly addressed by command, mention, or reply.
telegramConfig.forumAutoTopicHint: For forum-enabled supergroups, new top-level messages can automatically create a new topic that becomes the session container. This requires the bot to have admin or topic-management rights in that chat.

larkConfig.title: Configure Lark
larkConfig.subtitle: Set up your Lark/Feishu bot in a few steps
larkConfig.domainHint: Select Feishu if your organization uses feishu.cn, or Lark if it uses larksuite.com
larkConfig.step1Description: Create a self-built application on the Open Platform and add the Bot capability.
larkConfig.step1Item1: Go to the Open Platform and click "Create App"
larkConfig.step1Item2: Select "Enterprise Self-Built App" and fill in the app details
larkConfig.step2Description: Enter your App ID and App Secret from the "Credentials & Basic Info" page.
larkConfig.step2WsConnected: Connection established. You can now configure events in the next step.
larkConfig.appIdHint: Found in Credentials & Basic Info page
larkConfig.appSecretHint: Click to reveal and copy from the Open Platform
larkConfig.proxyUrlLarkLimitation: Heads-up: lark-oapi has no proxy hook today, so the SDK message path bypasses this proxy. Auth and direct REST calls honor it.
larkConfig.chatListHint: These are the groups the bot has joined. You can enable specific groups in the next step.
larkConfig.step3Description: In Permission Management, click "Batch Import/Export Permissions" to enable the following permissions.
larkConfig.step3Item4: im:message.group_msg — Receive all group messages (required for non-@mention mode)
larkConfig.step3Item10: im:message:update — Edit/update sent messages and cards
larkConfig.step3Item12: cardkit:card:write — Create and manage card templates
larkConfig.step3Item14: contact:user.base:readonly — Read user basic info (legacy scope)
larkConfig.step3BatchImport: Quick setup: Copy JSON and use "Batch Import/Export Permissions"
larkConfig.step3BatchImportHint: In Permission Management, click "Batch Import/Export Permissions", paste this JSON, and confirm.
larkConfig.step3ManualList: Or manually enable permissions one by one
larkConfig.step4Description: Subscribe to message events and card callbacks, then enable long connection so the bot can receive messages and respond to button interactions in real time.
larkConfig.step4Item2: Set subscription method to "Use Long Connection"
larkConfig.step4Item3: Click "Add Event" and subscribe to "Receive Messages im.message.receive_v1"
larkConfig.step4CallbackDesc: Callbacks handle card button clicks. Without this, menu buttons will not work.
larkConfig.step4CallbackItem2: Click "Add Callback" and add "Card Interaction card.action.trigger"
larkConfig.step4CallbackItem3: Set subscription method to "Use Long Connection" as well
larkConfig.step4Tip: Both events and callbacks must be configured, otherwise the bot cannot receive messages or respond to button actions. Remember to publish a new version after configuration.
larkConfig.step4LongConnFaqDesc: If you only see "Send events to developer server", your app may not have the Bot capability enabled. Go back to Step 1, enter your app → Add Capabilities → enable "Bot". After saving, return to Events & Callbacks and the "Use Long Connection" option will appear.
larkConfig.step4LarkWsWarning: Lark (International) may not have the WebSocket option. If it is unavailable, please use the domestic Feishu version instead.
larkConfig.step5Description: Create and publish a version to make the app available in your organization.
larkConfig.step5Item1: Create a new version and submit for release
larkConfig.step5Tip: After publishing, all configurations (permissions, events, bot capability) will take effect.

wechatConfig.title: WeChat Setup
wechatConfig.subtitle: Scan the QR code with your WeChat to connect.
wechatConfig.startDescription: Click the button below to generate a WeChat login QR code. You will scan it with your phone to connect.
wechatConfig.waitingForScan: Waiting for you to scan the QR code...
wechatConfig.confirmOnPhone: Scanned! Please confirm on your phone.
wechatConfig.verifyCodePrompt: Enter the verification code shown in WeChat.
wechatConfig.scanHint: Open WeChat on your phone and scan this QR code to log in.
wechatConfig.nextStepDesc: You can now send `/start` in WeChat to open the menu.
wechatConfig.qrExpired: QR code expired. Please try again.
wechatConfig.pollError: An error occurred while checking status.
wechatConfig.alreadyBoundDesc: Your WeChat account is already linked. You can proceed or rebind with a different account.

slackConfig.title: Slack Configuration
slackConfig.saasDescription: Authorize the official Slack app and wait for workspace binding.
slackConfig.selfHostDescription: Create a Slack app and configure tokens for Socket Mode.
slackConfig.oauthPrompt: Open OAuth in your browser to authorize Avibe with your Slack workspace.
slackConfig.afterAuth: After authorization, return here to continue.
slackConfig.step1Description: Click the button below to create a new Slack app with pre-configured permissions.
slackConfig.step1Tip: The manifest will be auto-filled. Just select your workspace and click Create.
slackConfig.step2Description: Install the app to your workspace and copy the Bot Token.
slackConfig.step2Item1: In your new app, go to 'OAuth & Permissions'
slackConfig.step3Description: Generate an App-Level Token for Socket Mode connection.
slackConfig.step3Warning: Keep this token secure. It provides full Socket Mode access.
slackConfig.step4Description: Validate your tokens to ensure everything is configured correctly.
slackConfig.step4Item3: The bot will now receive messages from this channel
slackConfig.step4Tip: You can also right-click a channel and select 'Add apps'.

agentDetection.title: Set up your agent backends
agentDetection.subtitle: We scan this machine for installed agents. Turn on the ones you have, and install anything that's missing.
agentDetection.detectedHelper: Detected automatically on this machine — enable what you have, install anything missing.
agentDetection.permissionHint: OpenCode requires permission config to skip approval prompts.
agentDetection.permissionHintStrong: Without this, OpenCode prompts for approval on every tool call. Avibe can't auto-reply, so the agent stalls.
agentDetection.permissionGateHint: Allow OpenCode tool calls above before you continue.
agentDetection.installHint: Agent not found. Click to install automatically.

backendLifecycle.title: Backend status
backendLifecycle.errorHint: {{name}} CLI is not installed. Install it from this card to enable the backend.
backendLifecycle.disabledHint: Toggle this backend on in the card header to activate.

channelList.title: Group Settings
channelList.subtitle: Enable groups and configure routing for each group.
channelList.inviteBotHint:
Invite the bot to your channel first:
1. Open the channel, click ⚙️ Settings → Integrations → Add apps
2. Or type /invite @YourBotName in the channel
channelList.discordInviteBotHint: Add the bot to your guild and grant access to the channel.
channelList.larkInviteBotHint: Add the bot to a group chat: Open the group → Settings → Bots → Add Bot.
channelList.telegramInviteBotHint: Send a message from the Telegram group or forum to the bot first, then refresh this list.
channelList.wechatInviteBotHint: Send a message to your WeChat bot account to start a conversation.
channelList.guildAccess: Guilds where the bot can work
channelList.telegramDiscoveryInfo: This page shows Telegram groups and forums that the bot has already seen from incoming messages. Direct messages stay hidden here.
channelList.telegramDiscoveryEmptyDesc: Send a message in any Telegram group or forum where the bot is present, then refresh this page.
channelList.accessPolicyHint: Groups are enabled only when checked below.
channelList.accessPolicyDiscord: Guilds and groups are two separate switches
channelList.accessPolicyHintDiscord: First choose the Discord guilds where the bot can work, then enable specific groups and routing below.
channelList.noChannelsFound: No groups found. Check token or invite bot.
channelList.addTokenFirst: Add a bot token first to load groups.
channelList.showMessageTypesHint:
Control which message types are shown in the conversation:

• Muttering: Agent responses and explanations
• Toolcall: Tool execution details (one-line summary)

Note: The agent's final result is always shown regardless of these settings.
channelList.groupDefaultsHint: Defaults for new groups on this platform. Each group can override its own value below.
channelList.unavailableSince: No longer returned by the platform since {{at}}
channelList.removeChannelConfirm: Permanently remove "{{name}}" and its settings? This cannot be undone.
channelList.discordNeedsGuild: Pick a Discord guild from the Discord tab to see channels here.
channelList.platformDisabled: Platform disabled — enable it in Settings to see groups.

summary.title: Review & Finish
summary.subtitle: Confirm settings and start Avibe.
summary.requireMentionHint: Bot only responds when @mentioned in groups
summary.autoUpdateHint: Automatically install updates when system is idle
summary.tipStartCommandDesc: Type /start in any enabled conversation to open the control panel.
summary.tipAgentSwitchDesc: Prefix your message with AgentName: to route to a specific agent. E.g., "Plan: Design the API"
summary.tipThreadDesc: Each thread is an isolated session. Start multiple threads for parallel tasks.
summary.bindCodeDesc: Send this command to the bot in a DM to become the first admin:

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.subtitle: Live status of your local Avibe node.
dashboard.metricCloudHintConfigured: Start the connector to make this node reachable
dashboard.metricCloudHintDisconnected: Recommended: enable cloud access for this node
dashboard.platformOverviewSubtitle: See which messaging platforms are live and jump to group routing where needed.
dashboard.platformNoGroupsHint: Direct messages only on this platform
dashboard.recentActivitySubtitle: Latest runtime events from the main service log.
dashboard.recentActivityEmpty: No recent service log events yet.
dashboard.groupsAndUsersHint: Review group routing and user access separately now that they have dedicated pages.
dashboard.settingsShortcutHint: Jump straight into Agent defaults or backend configuration.
dashboard.ackModeHint: How bot acknowledges received messages. Falls back to the next mode if the current one is unavailable.
dashboard.audioTranscriptionHint: Transcribe supported audio attachments before sending the turn to the agent
dashboard.audioTranscriptionRequiresVibeCloud: Pair this Avibe with Avibe Cloud before enabling audio transcription
dashboard.audioTranscriptionRequiresRemoteAccessTo: Open Remote Access and pair this device with avibe.bot to enable audio transcription.
dashboard.audioTranscriptEchoHint: Send recognized text back to the chat when transcription succeeds
dashboard.errorRetryLimitHint: Auto-retry with 'continue' when LLM stream errors occur (0 = no retry)
dashboard.showDurationHint: Display task elapsed time in result messages
dashboard.includeTimeInfoHint: Prepend the current local time, including seconds and UTC offset, to messages sent to agents
dashboard.includeUserInfoHint: Prepend [username<user_id>] to messages sent to agents
dashboard.replyEnhancementsHint: Allow agents to add clickable quick-reply buttons at the end of result messages
dashboard.showPagesPromptHint: Tell agents when and how to use visual Show Pages. The vibe show CLI remains available when this is off.
dashboard.agentProgressStyleHint: How agent progress is shown: off, a single concise status bubble that becomes the result, or the verbose process log.
dashboard.chatMessageFontSizeHint: Font size for user and agent message bubbles in the Web UI chat
dashboard.showAgentActivityHint: Show the agent's intermediate steps live in chat — interim messages and tool-call summaries; collapses automatically when the work finishes.
dashboard.showToolCallsHint: Show tool-call rows inside the Activity panel — turn off to keep only the agent's narration. Step counts and durations are unaffected. Also toggleable live from the panel's Tools control.
dashboard.slackLinkPreviewsHint: Disable Slack URL and media preview cards for messages sent by Avibe

logs.title: System Logs
logs.searchPlaceholder: Search logs by message, logger, or timestamp...

directoryBrowser.title: Select Project Folder
directoryBrowser.newFolderExists: A folder with that name already exists.
directoryBrowser.editPathPlaceholder: Absolute path, press Enter to go
directoryBrowser.pathNotFound: Path doesn't exist or isn't readable.

wechat.userBound: Your WeChat account is automatically configured as the bot owner.
wechat.noChannels: WeChat uses direct messaging — no group configuration needed.
wechat.setupComplete: WeChat bot connected! Your account has been automatically configured.

userList.title: Direct Messages
userList.subtitle: Bound users from each IM platform · per-user routing & admin rights.
userList.noUsers: No bound users yet. Create a bind code and share it with users.
userList.removeConfirm: Remove this user? This cannot be undone.
userList.lastAdminDemoteWarning: You are removing the last admin role. Continuing will put the bot into an 'unrestricted admin' state, where any bound user can access admin menus. Continue?
userList.lastAdminRemoveWarning: You are removing the last admin user. Continuing will put the bot into an 'unrestricted admin' state, where any bound user can access admin menus. Continue?

bindCode.title: Bind Codes
bindCode.inviteTitle: Invite a new user from any platform
bindCode.inviteDescription: Generate a one-time code. They DM your bot the code from Slack/Discord/Telegram/etc., and they're bound to this Avibe instance with the routing rules below.
bindCode.noActiveHint: Generate one to invite a user.
bindCode.noCodes: No bind codes yet. Create one to get started.
bindCode.usageTitle: How to use a bind code
bindCode.usageHint: Share an active code with the user, then ask them to DM the bot with `bind <code>`. `/bind <code>` still works too.
bindCode.noAvailableCodes: No active bind codes. Create a new code to start binding users.

agents.title: Agents
agents.subtitle: {{count}} agents registered across all backends.

running.unreachableBody: The Avibe controller is not responding. Start the service and try again.

graph.subtitle: Who started whom, who reports back — click a node for details, double-click to open the chat.
graph.empty: No sessions in this view yet.
graph.truncated: Showing the most recent 300 sessions — narrow the filters to see the rest.

orphans.title: {{count}} unattributed process(es)

detail.endGone: This session is no longer live.

agents.importNoneFound: No {{backend}} agents found on disk to import.
agents.renameKeptOld: Agent renamed (old one kept due to references)
agents.noConfig: No model or system prompt configured
agents.empty: No agents yet. Create one to start a conversation.
agents.selectPrompt: Select an agent to view its configuration.
agents.deleteConfirm: Delete agent "{{name}}"? This cannot be undone.
agents.deleteInUse: Agent "{{name}}" is currently in use by one or more channels and cannot be deleted.

detail.enabledHint: Disabling keeps the agent in the registry but excludes it from new sessions; running sessions are unaffected.
detail.defaultHint: New chats and IM messages without an explicit agent route to this one.
detail.defaultNeedsEnabled: Enable the agent before making it the default.
detail.name: Name
detail.modelEmpty: No matches — you can type a custom value.
detail.systemPromptEditorHint: Write the full system prompt with live Markdown preview.
detail.systemLocked: System agents cannot be deleted. You can still adjust model, effort, and system prompt.
detail.nameLocked: Used as the reference id · locked after create

runDialog.title: Open a chat with this agent
runDialog.description: Pick a project. A new session is created under it and the chat opens immediately.
runDialog.noProject: No projects yet — add one from the sidebar + button first.

create.title: New Agent
create.body: Pick a backend, name the agent, and fine-tune model and effort. Backend cannot be changed after creation.
create.name: Name
create.systemPromptPlaceholder: Optional. Prepended to every conversation that uses this agent.

agentsMd.title: Edit AGENTS.md
agentsMd.description: Edit this project's AGENTS.md — the project-level instructions handed to the agent.
agentsMd.placeholder: Write this project's AGENTS.md in Markdown…
agentsMd.loadedFromClaude: This project has no AGENTS.md yet — loaded CLAUDE.md's content as a starting point. Saving will write AGENTS.md.
agentsMd.symlinkToggle: Migrate CLAUDE.md into AGENTS.md and symlink them to stay in sync (recommended)
agentsMd.symlinkMigrateWarning: The current CLAUDE.md file will be deleted and replaced with a symlink to AGENTS.md.
agentsMd.savedSymlinkFailed: AGENTS.md saved, but the symlink could not be created.

projectSettings.title: Project settings
projectSettings.description: Configure where this project runs and which Agent its new sessions use.

workdir.label: Working directory
workdir.hint: The Agent works inside this directory.

defaultAgent.label: Default Agent
defaultAgent.hint: New sessions in this project start with this Agent, model, and reasoning effort. Leave it on "Follow global default" to use your global default Agent instead. Changing it never affects existing sessions.

guidance.label: Project guidance prompt
guidance.hint: Project-level instructions (AGENTS.md) handed to the Agent for every session in this project.
guidance.noFolder: Add a folder to this project to edit its guidance prompt.

globalPrompts.title: Global prompts
globalPrompts.description: Edit each backend's global instructions file — applied ahead of every agent's system prompt on this machine.
globalPrompts.placeholder: Write global instructions for this backend in Markdown…
globalPrompts.tipEffect: Prepended to the system prompt of every {{backend}} agent. New sessions pick it up.
globalPrompts.missingHint: this file doesn't exist yet — saving creates it
globalPrompts.readError: Couldn't read this file (not valid UTF-8). Fix it on disk before editing it here.
globalPrompts.syncConfirm: Overwrite all three backends' global prompt files with {{backend}}'s current content?
globalPrompts.discardConfirm: You have unsaved changes. Close and discard them?

skills.title: Skills
skills.subtitle: Manage global and project skills across your agent backends · powered by askill.
skills.empty: No skills installed yet. Add one from GitHub, a .zip, or the registry.
skills.notInstalledHint: askill powers the Skills page. Install it now, or it installs automatically when Avibe updates.
skills.projectNoFolder: This project has no folder configured, so it has no project-scoped skills — showing global skills only.
skills.removeConfirm: Remove skill "{{name}}"? This unlinks it from the selected scope.
skills.projectSectionGlobalHint: manage these in the Global tab
skills.globalAlsoAvailable: Global skills are available here too

addDialog.title: Add a skill
addDialog.subtitle: From a GitHub repo, or upload a local .zip package.
addDialog.dropzoneHint: Drag & drop a .zip here, or click to choose one.
addDialog.localNote: Unpacked and installed locally via askill — never sent to a third-party service.
addDialog.fetchFirst: Enter a source and fetch to see its skills.

browse.title: Browse the askill registry
browse.subtitle: AI-reviewed skills from askill.sh.
browse.showing: Showing {{shown}} of {{total}} · ranked by AI score

harness.title: Harness
harness.subtitle: Scheduled tasks, watches, webhooks, and the run history of all three.

bannerToggle.title: Background task banner
bannerToggle.description: Show in-progress background tasks at the bottom of chats.

harness.selectPrompt: Select a row to view its full configuration.
harness.emptyTasks: No scheduled tasks. Ask an agent to create one.
harness.emptyWatches: No watches running. Ask an agent to start one.
harness.emptyRuns: No runs yet. They appear here once a task or watch fires.
harness.webhooksSoonBody: Inbound HTTP triggers will land in a follow-up release. Tasks and watches cover scheduled and event-driven runs today.

row.deleteConfirmWatch: Delete "{{name}}"? The watch will stop immediately.

createDialog.title: Talk to an agent to set up background work
createDialog.subtitle: Writing cron is the tool era; describing intent is the colleague era.
createDialog.body: Scheduled tasks, watches, and (soon) webhooks all come from a chat with an agent. The agent translates your intent into vibe task add / vibe watch add arguments so you never have to remember cron syntax.
createDialog.example: Example: "Every weekday at 9:30am, summarize yesterday's #ops activity and post to #ops." The agent picks the cron, builds the prompt, and binds it to this project.
createDialog.noProject: No projects yet — add one from the sidebar + button first.
createDialog.open: Open chat and walk me through
createDialog.promptTask: Help me create a scheduled task. What I want: [describe here, e.g. "every weekday at 9:30am summarize yesterday's PR comments"]. Once parameters look right, run vibe task add and bind it to this project.
createDialog.promptWatch: Help me create a watch. I want to wait for: [describe here, e.g. "CI workflow #1234 to finish"]. Notify me when the condition is met. Once parameters look right, run vibe watch add.

selection.askFailed: Couldn't start a new session from the selection.

annotate.smartDesc: Click elements · select text · box a region

showPage.title: Show Page
showPage.prompt: Please visualize this session as a Show Page.
showPage.publicDesc: Anyone with the link can view
showPage.offlineNote: This Show Page is offline and can't be shared right now.
showPage.publicUnavailable: Connect Avibe Cloud to get a public link others can open.
showPage.pinToDockCaption: Opens as an app from the Dock
showPage.addToHomeBodySafari: Tap here to open in a new tab, then Share → “Add to Home Screen”.
showPage.addToHomeBodyPwa: Copy the link above and open it in Safari, then tap Share → “Add to Home Screen”.

chat.noAgents: No enabled agents. Create one on the Agents page.
chat.changesPersist: changes apply on the next reply
chat.sessionOverrideHint: New sessions inherit the project's defaults; changes here apply immediately and only to this session.
chat.missingSessionId: Missing session id in the URL.
chat.transcriptEmpty: Type a message below to start the conversation
chat.forkedEmptyBody: This session has been forked from the source context. The visible transcript starts clean, and the source context is preserved so you can continue the conversation here.
chat.turnInProgress: A turn is already running — wait for the reply.
chat.stopFailed: Couldn't reach the agent to stop it — it may still be running.

compose.placeholderBusy: Type to queue while the agent works…

agentActivity.label: Activity
agentActivity.empty: No activity recorded for this turn.

vaults.title: Vaults
vaults.subtitle: Give your AI agents secrets — their values never enter the context.
vaults.empty: No secrets yet. Add one — the agent retrieves it via the CLI without the value touching the chat.

filter.empty: No secrets match the selected tags.

grants.title: Active access
grants.subtitle: Grants agents can use right now — revoke to cut access immediately.
grants.revokeConfirm: Revoke access to "{{target}}" now? Agents lose it immediately.

deleteDialog.title: Delete "{{name}}"?
deleteDialog.description: Agents will no longer be able to use this secret.
deleteDialog.keypairIrreversible: This is a signing key — deleting it permanently destroys the private key. There is no backup and it cannot be recovered.
deleteDialog.keypairFunds: If this key's address holds any funds, move them out first, or they will be lost for good.
deleteDialog.protectedUnlockNote: This is a protected secret — deleting the record does not reveal its value, but agents will lose access immediately.

lock.windowTooltip: Unlock window — while unlocked, approvals don't re-ask for your passkey; signing always does. Lock now to wipe the key from this browser.

settings.title: Vault settings
settings.subtitle: How long the vault stays unlocked, and how strictly approvals ask.
settings.unlockWindowHelp: How long the vault stays unlocked in this browser after you unlock it. Applies the next time you unlock.
settings.strictHelp: Ask for your passkey on every approval and reveal — not just an in-sandbox confirmation. Signing always asks regardless. Turning this on re-locks the vault so it takes effect right away.

errors.contextFailed: Couldn't prepare the reveal. Please try again.
errors.unavailable: Reveal isn't available for this secret yet.

edit.title: Edit info
edit.subtitle: Info edits keep grants; fetch policy changes require new approval
edit.lockedHint: Name, kind and protection can't be changed

protectedUnlock.unlocked: Protected vault unlocked for this session
protectedUnlock.setupSubtitle: Unlocked only by you, in your browser. Even a stolen machine can't open them.
protectedUnlock.unlockSubtitle: {{name}} is protected — unlock it in your browser to continue
protectedUnlock.passkeyCaption: Face ID · Touch ID · security key — the most secure option
protectedUnlock.unlockPasskeyCaption: Face ID · Touch ID · security key
protectedUnlock.factorNote: Your passkey proof never leaves the browser — the machine alone can't decrypt this
protectedUnlock.passkeyUnrecoverableWarning: This passkey is the only key. Lose it and the protected secrets can't be recovered — there is no backup.
protectedUnlock.passkeyAck: I understand: if I lose this passkey, these secrets can't be recovered.
protectedUnlock.unlockUnavailableHere: Protected vaults need a passkey. Open Vaults from localhost or your avibe.bot address; raw IP addresses like 127.0.0.1 can't use WebAuthn.

errors.prfUnavailable: This passkey/browser doesn't support the PRF extension needed for encryption. Try a platform passkey (Touch ID / Windows Hello).
errors.noPasskey: No passkey is configured for this protected vault.
errors.discoveryFailed: Couldn't load your protected vault. Check your connection and retry — don't set up a new one, or you'll split your vault keys.
errors.alreadyInitialized: This vault was just set up elsewhere. Reload and unlock with your existing passkey.

dialog.title: Add a secret
dialog.subtitle: Stored encrypted; the value never reaches the chat or the model.
dialog.name: Name
dialog.provisionValueHelp: Submitted over TLS — never enters the chat or the model context
dialog.kindStaticHelp: A token, password, or any text value.
dialog.kindKeypairHelp: A signing key generated in your browser.
dialog.nameHint: Start with a letter or underscore; then use letters, numbers, or underscore · case preserved
dialog.signingKeyHelp: A new secp256k1 signing key is created in your browser. The private key is sealed in your browser — standard keys are signed by avault locally, protected keys are signed in your browser — and only the public key is stored in the clear.
dialog.signingKeyProtectedHelp: A new secp256k1 signing key is generated inside the sandbox and sealed there. Only the ciphertext and public addresses reach Avibe — the private key never leaves the sandbox.
dialog.signingKeyProtectedBornHint: The private key was born inside the sandbox and never entered this app.
dialog.signingImportPlaceholder: Paste a 32-byte hex private key (64 hex chars)
dialog.signingAddressesUnavailable: Couldn't derive addresses for this key.
dialog.signingAddressesHint: Derived from the key. Share an address to receive funds; the private key never leaves your vault, and the chain and signature scheme are chosen at signing time.
dialog.signingNeedsAvault: Signing keys need avault {{version}} or newer installed.
dialog.p2Unavailable: Browser-side vault create requires avault {{version}} or newer. Installed: {{installed}}. Install a compatible avault release before saving standard secrets.
dialog.browserSealingPending: Browser-side sealing requires a compatible avault release. This UI never sends plaintext values to Python.
dialog.allowHostsHelp: Only needed when this secret is used through the brokered HTTP proxy; '.example.com' matches subdomains.
dialog.fetchAuthHelp: Defaults to Authorization: Bearer <value>. Use Header or Query for APIs that expect another credential placement.
dialog.tagsHelp: Labels to find and group your secrets.
dialog.protectedHelp: The agent can't use it on its own. It's unlocked only after you decrypt it in your browser with your passkey (end-to-end encrypted). Safe even if this computer is stolen — good for crypto wallets and any important secret.
dialog.standardHelp: The agent can use it on its own. Good for API keys, environment variables, and most secrets.
dialog.alwaysAskSignHelp: Standard keys can sign on their own. For a high-value key, turn this on so every signature needs your approval in the browser.
dialog.protectedCreatePending: Protected web create is not available in this build yet. Choose Standard only for secrets the agent may need to use on its own.

errors.aadFieldTooLarge: The sealed vault request is too large.
errors.invalidPublicKey: avault returned an invalid public key. Repair the avault dependency and try again.
errors.invalidPrivateKey: That isn't a valid secp256k1 private key. It must be 32 bytes (64 hex characters).
errors.fingerprintMismatch: The avault public key fingerprint changed. Verify the local avault install before saving this secret.
errors.pendingDraft: Press Enter to add the value in the input, or clear it, before saving.
errors.authNameRequired: Enter a header or query parameter name.
errors.authHeaderInvalid: Enter a valid HTTP header name.
errors.authHeaderForbidden: Host cannot be used as the credential header.
errors.authQueryInvalid: Enter a valid query parameter name.
errors.secretExists: A secret with this name already exists.
errors.secretNameCaseConflict: A secret already uses this name with different letter casing.

requests.title: Pending requests
requests.subtitle: An agent is waiting — approve or deny from this device.

approval.accessTitle: Agent wants to use a secret
approval.accessSubtitle: You decide whether the agent may use it. Its value is never shown to the AI or the chat.
approval.signSubtitle: Each signature needs your approval — it can't be pre-approved.
approval.grantDurationHelp: How long the agent may use the secret after you approve.
approval.grantOneTimeHelp: The agent completes this one delivery, then access ends.
approval.grantOneShotHelp: This request is one-time only — the agent gets a single delivery, so the duration is fixed.
approval.accessNote: Unlocked in your browser — the secret's value is never shown to the AI or the chat.
approval.signNote: The private key never leaves your browser — only the signature is returned.

errors.failed: Couldn't complete the approval. Please try again.
errors.loadFailed: Couldn't load this request. It may have been handled already.
errors.noScope: This request has no grantable scope.
errors.missingDigest: This sign request is missing its digest.
errors.missingMaterial: Protected key material wasn't available to sign. Reload and try again.

request.title: Agent needs a secret
request.help: Provide the value — it goes to the vault, never to the chat or model.
request.ambiguousProvision: Multiple pending requests need this secret. Open the request-specific Provide secret row in Vaults so the requested details are preserved.
request.saveFailed: Couldn't save the secret. Please try again.
